question: How do the 14 major critical arguments against the three angels' messages fare when tested against biblical evidence? Take each objection and respond using evidence from the prior 19 studies.
prior_research_summary: This is study 20 of 21 in the 3AM series. All 19 prior studies are complete (3am-00 through 3am-19). The reference agent gathered findings from every prior study mapped to each of the 14 objections, plus external corpus leads from EGW, Secrets Unsealed, and YouTube critics (Answering Adventism). Web research identified the strongest formulations of each objection from three critical traditions: former-Adventist (Ratzlaff, Tinker), evangelical/Reformed (Packer, Ortlund, GotQuestions), and academic/internal (Ford, deSilva, Spectrum). The strongest objections are: entole vs. nomos (Obj 4/6), Ford's Glacier View arguments (Obj 8), Sabbath silence (Obj 6), ECT arguments (Obj 7), and Gal 1:8-9 application (Obj 2). The weakest are: no gospel (Obj 1), literal angels (Obj 11), and 19th-century invention (Obj 14).

focus_areas.title: Objection 1-2: Is the everlasting gospel THE gospel or 'another gospel'?
focus_areas.what: Determine whether the content of Rev 14:6-7 matches or contradicts the Pauline gospel
focus_areas.why: These objections claim the 3AM either lacks gospel content (natural theism) or adds to Paul's gospel (Gal 1:8-9 anathema)
focus_areas.how: Compare Rev 14:6-7 content with Paul's multiple gospel summaries. Parse the legon participle in Rev 14:7. Check the basar-euangelizo LXX chain.
focus_areas.narrative: READ $WORD_STUDIES/G2098-euangelion.md and $WORD_STUDIES/G2097-euangelizo.md and TR-basar-euangelizo.md for the gospel vocabulary chain. The critical question is whether Paul's gospel is limited to 1 Cor 15:3-4 or broader — check Rom 1:1-4, Acts 17:22-31, Gal 3:8 for Paul's OWN variant summaries. Run greek_parser.py --verse on Rev 14:6-7 to confirm the legon participle embeds v.7 as gospel CONTENT. Compare Acts 17:22-31 element-by-element with Rev 14:6-7 (Creator God, universal scope, judgment, moral reorientation). Check Gal 1:6 for heteros (qualitatively different) — does Rev 14 add works-righteousness (the specific thing Paul condemns) or creation-worship? Retrieve Ecc 12:13-14 as the OT structural twin. Ratzlaff conflates Rev 14's angels with EGW's 'accompanying angel' — the research agent should note this conflation but focus on the biblical text.

focus_areas.title: Objection 3: Do the apostles teach these elements as gospel?
focus_areas.what: Verify whether NT apostolic preaching contains the three-part pattern (creation-worship, judgment, commandment-keeping) as gospel content
focus_areas.why: Critics claim no apostle taught 'fear God, the hour of judgment is come' as gospel
focus_areas.how: Trace the three-part pattern through John the Baptist, Jesus, Stephen, Paul, and other apostolic preaching
focus_areas.narrative: Three independent NT texts explicitly label the combined message — including judgment — as gospel: Luke 3:18 (euengelizeto for John's judgment preaching), Rom 2:16 ('according to my gospel'), Rev 14:6-7 (judgment IS the everlasting gospel content). Paul at Athens (Acts 17:22-31) contains the tightest correspondence with Rev 14:6-7. Run cross_testament_parallels_v2.py --hybrid-nt on Rev 14:7 to find NT parallels. READ $WORD_STUDIES/WG-gospel.md for comprehensive gospel vocabulary. Check whether Carson (Reformed, non-Adventist) acknowledged Acts 17's creation-judgment framework as 'the necessary framework without which his proclamation of Jesus makes no sense.' Prior study 3am-03 documented six major NT preaching episodes with the three-part structure.

focus_areas.title: Objection 4: Does Rev 14:12 teach legalism?
focus_areas.what: Determine whether the commandment-keeping + faith description constitutes works-righteousness
focus_areas.why: This is the most persistent charge against the 3AM — that adding commandment-keeping to faith equals legalism
focus_areas.how: Parse Rev 14:12 grammar (terountes participle = descriptive vs. imperative). Establish which commandments entole refers to. Trace the faith-love-law chain.
focus_areas.narrative: READ $WORD_STUDIES/G1785-entole.md and $WORD_STUDIES/G3551-nomos.md and TR-mitsvah-entole.md and TR-towrah-nomos.md for the commandment/law vocabulary analysis. The grammar is decisive: terountes is a present active PARTICIPLE describing the saints, not an imperative prescribing conditions. V.7 uses imperatives (commands); v.12 uses participle (description). READ $GRAMMAR_REF/greek/participle-present-active.md and $GRAMMAR_REF/greek/participle-legon-discourse.md. Three NT test cases demolish the entole-cannot-mean-Decalogue claim: Rom 7:7-12 (tenth commandment called entole 5x), Eph 6:2 (fifth commandment called entole), Luke 23:56 (Sabbath called entole). For the pistis Iesou genitive, READ $WORD_STUDIES/G4102-pistis.md and check $GRAMMAR_REF/greek/subjective-objective-genitive.md. The faith-love-law chain: Gal 5:6 -> 1 John 5:3 -> Rom 13:10 -> Rom 8:4 -> Rom 3:31. Also check WG-commandment-keeping.md, WG-commandment.md, WG-faith.md, WG-obedience.md, and WG-law.md.

focus_areas.title: Objection 5: Does 'fear God' conflict with 'perfect love casts out fear'?
focus_areas.what: Resolve the apparent contradiction between Rev 14:7 ('fear God') and 1 John 4:18 ('perfect love casteth out fear')
focus_areas.why: Critics argue these are irreconcilable — the angel commands fear while John says love eliminates it
focus_areas.how: Distinguish types of fear in Hebrew and Greek. Parse 1 John 4:17-18 for kolasis-fear. Check Exo 20:20 paradigm.
focus_areas.narrative: READ $WORD_STUDIES/G5399-phobeo.md, $WORD_STUDIES/G5401-phobos.md, $WORD_STUDIES/G2851-kolasis.md, $WORD_STUDIES/H3372-yare.md, $WORD_STUDIES/H3374-yirah.md, TR-yare-phobeo.md, and WG-fear.md. Three Hebrew words for fear: yare/yirah (reverential awe, 414 OT), eimah (terror), pachad (dread). LXX collapses all into phobos. The key text is 1 John 4:18 which specifies kolasis-fear — dread of divine punishment. Kolasis (G2851) appears only 2x in NT. Context (v.17) names 'boldness in the day of judgment.' Run greek_parser.py --verse on 1 John 4:17-18 to confirm the kolasis qualifier. The Exo 20:20 paradigm is decisive: 'Fear not' (stop the terror) AND 'that his fear may be before your faces' (maintain the reverence) — same root y-r-', two opposite commands in ONE verse. 1 Pet 1:17 tells believers who call God 'Father' to live 'in fear.' Ps 130:4 — forgiveness produces fear.

focus_areas.title: Objection 6: Is the Sabbath connection to Rev 14:7 just inference?
focus_areas.what: Evaluate whether Rev 14:7 alludes to the fourth commandment (Exo 20:11) or merely echoes generic creation theology
focus_areas.why: The word 'Sabbath' never appears in Revelation. Critics argue the creation language is generic, not commandment-specific
focus_areas.how: Compare the LXX of Exo 20:11 with Rev 14:7 word-by-word. Check verb diagnostic (poieo vs. ktizo). Examine the uniqueness of the divergent fourth element.
focus_areas.narrative: READ $WORD_STUDIES/G4160-poieo.md, $WORD_STUDIES/G3772-ouranos.md, $WORD_STUDIES/G1093-ge.md, $WORD_STUDIES/G2281-thalassa.md, $WORD_STUDIES/G4520-sabbatismos.md, $WORD_STUDIES/H7676-shabbath.md, TR-asah-poieo.md, TR-shamayim-ouranos.md, TR-shabbath-sabbaton.md, WG-sabbath.md, and WG-worship.md. Four terms in sequence match Exo 20:11 LXX: poieo (made), ouranos (heaven), ge (earth), thalassa (sea). Every OTHER creation text in Revelation uses ktizo (4:11, 10:6) — Rev 14:7 ALONE switches to poieo, mapping to asah in Exo 20:11. Every other biblical text echoing Exo 20:11 (Ps 146:6, Acts 4:24, Acts 14:15, Neh 9:6) concludes with 'all that is in them' — Rev 14:7 is the ONLY text to substitute 'fountains of waters.' READ $WORD_STUDIES/G4077-pege.md for the fountains divergence. The commandment inclusio: Rev 11:19 (ark) -> 12:17 (commandments) -> 14:7 (creation formula) -> 14:12 (commandments). Heb 4:9 sabbatismos is an NT hapax denoting Sabbath-keeping in every extant Greek source. This IS an allusion, not a quotation — honest acknowledgment required.

focus_areas.title: Objection 7: Does 'forever and ever' torment contradict annihilationism?
focus_areas.what: Determine whether Rev 14:11's 'smoke ascendeth up for ever and ever' requires eternal conscious torment or permits conditional immortality
focus_areas.why: ECT proponents (Packer, Ortlund) argue the plain language demands unending punishment. This is one of the strongest objections.
focus_areas.how: Check OT prophetic convention for 'forever' imagery. Compare Revelation's own parallel usage. Examine subject differentiation in Rev 20. Trace genre distribution.
focus_areas.narrative: READ $WORD_STUDIES/G165-aion.md, $WORD_STUDIES/G166-aionios.md, $WORD_STUDIES/H5769-owlam.md, TR-owlam-aion.md, WG-forever.md, WG-smoke.md, $WORD_STUDIES/G2372-thumos.md, $WORD_STUDIES/G3709-orge.md, TR-chemah-thymos.md, TR-ashan-kapnos.md, WG-death.md, WG-destroy.md, WG-perish-save.md, and WG-incorruptible.md. Isa 34:9-10 uses the STRONGEST Hebrew 'forever' vocabulary for Edom — yet Isa 34:11-17 describes animals dwelling in its ruins. Rev 19:3 applies the IDENTICAL smoke formula to Babylon whose cessation is documented five times with ou me...eti. Rev 20:9 uses katephagen (devoured) for human wicked; Rev 20:10 uses torment only for devil/beast/false prophet. Mal 4:1-3 — wicked become ashes. Every DIDACTIC NT passage uses destruction vocabulary; torment vocabulary appears only in apocalyptic framework. READ $WORD_STUDIES/G2851-kolasis.md (kolasis = 2x only, pruning/corrective). 1 Cor 15:24-28, Rev 21:4, Nah 1:9, 1 Tim 6:16 require evil's complete cessation. Packer's argument that same aionios must mean same duration — check Matt 25:46 parallel and the OT convention response.

focus_areas.title: Objection 8: Does the investigative judgment undermine assurance?
focus_areas.what: Evaluate whether pre-advent judgment necessarily robs believers of certainty of salvation
focus_areas.why: Ford's Glacier View arguments are the most theologically sophisticated critique. The question has 'haunted Adventists for 40+ years.'
focus_areas.how: Distinguish krisis from katakrima. Examine Dan 7:22 benefactive judgment. Trace five biblical grounds of assurance. Check the courtroom pattern.
focus_areas.narrative: READ $WORD_STUDIES/G2920-krisis.md, $WORD_STUDIES/G2631-katakrima.md, WG-judgment.md, $WORD_STUDIES/G968-bema.md, and TR-riyb-krisis.md. The krisis/katakrima distinction is critical: krisis (47x) = judicial evaluation; katakrima (3x) = adverse verdict + execution. Rom 8:1 negates katakrima, not krisis. Believers face evaluation but not condemnation. Dan 7:22 — Aramaic dina yehib leqaddishe = judgment given TO/FOR the saints (benefactive le preposition). Five grounds of assurance through judgment: (1) 1 John 5:13 oida certainty, (2) Eph 1:13-14 arrabon guarantee, (3) Rom 8:34 present tense intercession, (4) Mal 3:16-17 book of remembrance for believers, (5) Rom 5:9-10 a fortiori. The Zech 3:1-5 courtroom pattern: real guilt acknowledged, adversary accuses, divine advocate intervenes, cleansing, new status. Rom 8:33-34 replays: no prosecutor. Rev 12:10 — accuser permanently disbarred. Ford's critique is strongest on timing (1844), weakest on CONCEPT of pre-advent judgment which is built into Dan 7's structure (vv.9-10 before vv.13-14 before v.27).

focus_areas.title: Objection 9: Is 'close of probation' a biblical concept?
focus_areas.what: Determine whether Scripture teaches a pre-advent cessation of salvific opportunity
focus_areas.why: Critics argue the term is unbiblical, the concept contradicts God's character, and Rev 22:11 refers to the second coming itself
focus_areas.how: Examine Rev 22:11 in sequence (before 'I come quickly'). Check Rev 15:8 temple filling. Trace the shut-door motif. Examine graduated accountability.
focus_areas.narrative: The convergence of three independent texts is compelling: Rev 22:11 (destinies fixed BEFORE 'I come quickly' in v.12), Rev 15:8 (temple filled with smoke, no man able to enter = intercession ceases), Dan 12:1 (Michael stands up, deliverance for those found written in the book). READ $WORD_STUDIES/WG-rest.md and $WORD_STUDIES/G5281-hupomone.md. Biblical precedents: Gen 7:16 (God shut Noah's door SEVEN DAYS before the flood), Matt 25:10 (door shut), Luke 13:25 (master shuts door). The graduated accountability principle (Luke 12:47-48, Acts 17:30, 1 Tim 1:13) addresses the character concern — standard of judgment matches revelation received. 2 Thess 2:10-12 — God sends delusion BECAUSE they refused truth first (anth' hon). Run greek_parser.py --verse on Rev 22:11 and Rev 15:8 to establish grammatical structure. The critical point: the concept is biblical even though the TERM 'close of probation' is not.

focus_areas.title: Objection 10: Is the seal purely spiritual (Holy Spirit) with no commandment content?
focus_areas.what: Evaluate whether Revelation's seal imagery adds specific content beyond the Pauline Holy-Spirit-as-seal concept
focus_areas.why: Evangelicals argue the seal = the Holy Spirit's indwelling, period. No practice (Sabbath) is the seal.
focus_areas.how: Trace the owth (sign) designation across the Sabbath texts. Check Rom 4:11 sign/seal vocabulary bridge. Examine forehead-only vs. forehead-or-hand distinction. Analyze the three seal elements.
focus_areas.narrative: READ $WORD_STUDIES/H226-owth.md, $WORD_STUDIES/G4972-sphragizo.md, $WORD_STUDIES/G4973-sphragis.md, $WORD_STUDIES/G5480-charagma.md, $WORD_STUDIES/H2856-chatham.md, $WORD_STUDIES/H8420-tav.md, TR-owth-semeion.md, WG-sealing.md, WG-sign.md, and WG-mark.md. Sabbath is the ONLY Decalogue commandment called owth (sign) — four times (Exo 31:13, 31:17, Ezek 20:12, 20:20). Rom 4:11 breaks the sign/seal vocabulary barrier: Paul calls circumcision BOTH semeion AND sphragis. The fourth commandment uniquely contains three elements of a royal seal: name (YHWH), title (Creator), domain (heaven, earth, sea). Holy Spirit is the sealing AGENT (Eph 1:13, 4:30), not a competing alternative — 2 Cor 1:22 separates sealing from the Spirit-as-earnest. Forehead-only (God: 4 refs) vs. forehead-or-hand (beast: 3 refs with e disjunction). Ezek 9:4-6 mark on foreheads connects to the tav (H8420). The argument IS composite and inferential — honest acknowledgment required.

focus_areas.title: Objection 11-12: Literal vs. symbolic angels and the imperial euangelia parallel
focus_areas.what: Evaluate whether the angels are literal future beings or symbolic of human proclamation, and whether the imperial euangelion parallel undermines gospel content
focus_areas.why: Dispensationalists place the angels in a future tribulation; DeSilva's 'accession announcement' reading could reduce the gospel to anti-imperialism
focus_areas.how: Check genre indicators in Rev 12-14. Evaluate DeSilva's aionios translation against 2 Tim 1:9 and Matt 25:46.
focus_areas.narrative: READ $WORD_STUDIES/WG-angel.md and $WORD_STUDIES/G166-aionios.md. The literary structure is decisive: symbolic antagonists (beasts) demand symbolic protagonists (angels). The fourfold universal formula (tribe, people, tongue, nation) in Rev 13:7 matches Rev 14:6 word for word. The semeion inclusio (12:1, 12:3, 15:1) brackets the unit. The hupomone bracket (13:10 and 14:12) makes this a single literary unit. READ $WORD_STUDIES/G5281-hupomone.md and $GRAMMAR_REF/greek/participle-legon-discourse.md. DeSilva's 'age-long accession announcement' reading is a real insight about counter-imperial vocabulary but fails as PRIMARY meaning: his 'age-long' translation of aionios breaks at 2 Tim 1:9 (grace given 'before eternal times') and Matt 25:46 (same adjective for punishment and life). The imperial parallel can SUPPORT the reading — the gospel has cosmic-political dimensions — without replacing soteriological content.

focus_areas.title: Objection 13: Is Babylon exclusively Rome or a past entity?
focus_areas.what: Determine whether Babylon in Revelation can be confined to first-century Rome or must be a broader religious system
focus_areas.why: Preterists identify Babylon with pagan Rome; historicists with the papacy. If Babylon is past, the second angel's message has no ongoing application.
focus_areas.how: Examine the zanah/porneia covenant requirement. Check the bride-harlot bracket. Analyze METER TON PORNON structure. Test the Rome-rides-Rome problem.
focus_areas.narrative: READ $WORD_STUDIES/H2181-zanah.md, $WORD_STUDIES/G4202-porneia.md, $WORD_STUDIES/G4204-porne.md, $WORD_STUDIES/H1101-balal.md, TR-zanah-porne.md, and WG-fornication.md. The harlotry vocabulary is decisive: zanah (107x OT) in the prophets is used almost exclusively for entities that were ONCE faithful. Rome never married YHWH. Rev 17:1-3 and 21:9-10 share the same angel-introduction formula word-for-word — if the bride (New Jerusalem) is symbolic, the harlot must also be symbolic. METER TON PORNON (Rev 17:5) = the SOURCE generating other harlots — cannot be confined to any one entity. The balal etymology points to MIXTURE (truth with error). The Rome-rides-Rome problem: if both beast and harlot are Rome, Rome rides upon Rome, hates Rome, burns Rome. The priestly-garment parody in Rev 17:4 matches the high priest's garments minus blue (tekeleth, Num 15:38-39 — the commandment color). Run cross_testament_parallels_v2.py on Rev 17:1-5.

focus_areas.title: Objection 14: Are the 3AM a 19th-century Adventist invention?
focus_areas.what: Test whether the three-part pattern (gospel call, exposure of false system, judgment warning) predates the Millerite movement
focus_areas.why: This is a genetic fallacy but still requires answer — if the pattern exists only in Rev 14 as interpreted by 19th-century Adventists, it may reflect eisegesis
focus_areas.how: Trace the three-part pattern through OT and NT episodes. Check lexical bridges from Hebrew to Greek through LXX.
focus_areas.narrative: READ TR-riyb-krisis.md, TR-shachah-proskyneo.md, and TR-basar-euangelizo.md for the lexical bridges. Prior study 3am-02 documented six major OT episodes with the same three-part pattern: Noah, Sodom, Egypt, Carmel, Josiah, Jeremiah. Prior study 3am-03 documented the NT pattern in John the Baptist (Matt 3:2-12), Jesus (Matt 23), Stephen (Acts 7), Paul at Athens (Acts 17), Paul in Romans (Rom 1-3). The covenant lawsuit (riyb) tradition provides the formal legal structure. Consistent Hebrew vocabulary traces into the Greek of Revelation through the LXX: riyb to krisis, shachah to proskyneo, yatsa to exerchomai, chemah to thumos. Many biblical truths were rediscovered at specific historical moments (justification by faith in the Reformation) — the date of discovery does not determine the validity of the interpretation. The question is whether the TEXT supports it.

focus_areas.title: Honest tensions and difficult passages
focus_areas.what: Identify where the biblical response is genuinely strong, where it requires nuance, and where legitimate tension remains
focus_areas.why: CUSTOM-INSTRUCTIONS.md requires honest engagement — noting where answers are strong, nuanced, or tension exists
focus_areas.how: Catalog the strength of each answer. Note where inference is required. Acknowledge what critics get right.
focus_areas.narrative: The analysis agent should rate each objection response: STRONG (Objections 1, 3, 5, 11, 14 — text clearly supports the Adventist reading or critics' own scholarship agrees), NUANCED (Objections 4, 6, 10 — the answer requires careful linguistic work and honest acknowledgment that the Sabbath is NOT named in Revelation and the seal argument IS composite and inferential), and TENSION (Objections 7, 8, 9 — legitimate exegetical tension exists, particularly on the IJ timing question where Ford's critique is strongest, the ECT arguments where the same aionios applies to both punishment and life, and the close-of-probation concept where the term is indeed unbiblical even though the concept has support). The analysis must not dismiss critics by attacking their motives — engage their arguments. Note where the three critical traditions (former-Adventist, evangelical, academic) converge and where they contradict each other.

NT parallels for pre-advent judgment scene
NT parallels for creation formula (to compare with Rev 14:7)

grammar_references.description: Present participle legon as discourse connector (Rev 14 structure)
Subjective vs. objective genitive (pistis Iesou debate)
Divine passive (passivum divinum) — edothe in Revelation

external_corpus_leads.claim: Galatians 1:8-9 applies to the angel of Rev 14 because 3AM is 'another gospel' adding works
external_corpus_leads.directive: Verify by parsing Gal 1:6 heteros and testing whether Rev 14:7 content matches Paul's Athens sermon (Acts 17:22-31)

external_corpus_leads.claim: entole in John = Jesus' teachings only, never Decalogue; nomos = Law
external_corpus_leads.directive: Test against Rom 7:7-12 (Paul uses entole for 10th commandment), Eph 6:2 (5th commandment = entole), Luke 23:56 (Sabbath = entole)

external_corpus_leads.claim: IJ denies finality of cross, God's omniscience, reality of saving faith
external_corpus_leads.directive: Verify krisis vs katakrima distinction (Rom 8:1), Dan 7:22 benefactive le, five NT grounds of assurance

external_corpus_leads.claim: aionios must mean same duration for punishment as for life — differential interpretation is 'evasive'
external_corpus_leads.directive: Check OT prophetic convention where identical 'forever' language describes temporal destructions (Isa 34:9-10). Check Rev 19:3 parallel.

external_corpus_leads.claim: 'No rest day and night' in Rev 14:11 requires conscious ongoing torment
external_corpus_leads.directive: Compare with Rev 4:8 where identical 'rest not day and night' construction describes living creatures' ceaseless praise. Check tense shift.

external_corpus_leads.claim: 'The Sabbath is not the seal' — no verse says this verbatim
external_corpus_leads.directive: Acknowledge this is technically correct, then present the composite argument: owth designation (4 texts), sign/seal vocabulary bridge (Rom 4:11), three seal elements in 4th commandment only

external_corpus_leads.claim: euangelion aionion = 'age-long accession announcement' paralleling Roman imperial propaganda
external_corpus_leads.directive: Test 'age-long' translation of aionios against 2 Tim 1:9 and Matt 25:46. Can the parallel enhance rather than replace soteriological content?

external_corpus_leads.claim: No mention of grace, faith, hope, love, forgiveness, salvation, or cross in the angel's messages
external_corpus_leads.directive: Check whether this is a critique of the TEXT or of Adventist PRESENTATION. Does euangelion aionion itself contain/presuppose grace?

external_corpus_leads.claim: Babylon = first-century Rome exclusively, making historicist application anachronistic
external_corpus_leads.directive: Check zanah/porneia covenant requirement (H2181 requires prior faithfulness), bride-harlot bracket (Rev 17:1-3 ‖ 21:9-10), Rome-rides-Rome problem

external_corpus_leads.claim: Three angels terminology is purely biblical — Rev 14:6-12 explicitly numbers them
external_corpus_leads.directive: Verify by reading Rev 14:6, 14:8, 14:9 for explicit 'another angel' numbering

external_corpus_leads.claim: Rev 14:7 is gospel-producing proper fear of God, not natural religion
external_corpus_leads.directive: Note as historical Reformed agreement with the Adventist reading — the fear-God command IS gospel, not alternative to gospel

research_instructions:
This is a SYNTHESIS study. The prior 19 studies (3am-00 through 3am-19) have already answered each of the 14 objections with specific biblical evidence. The research agent's primary task is to:

1. READ the prior study conclusions listed in 00-references.md — these contain the detailed verse analysis already completed.
2. Retrieve the KEY verses that answer each objection — not all verses from all prior studies, but the decisive ones.
3. READ the extensive word-studies library entries — nearly every relevant Strong's number already has a canonical study.
4. Verify external corpus claims from Ratzlaff, Tinker, Ford, Packer, DeSilva, and YouTube critics against the biblical evidence.
5. Run parsers on the 10 specified verses to confirm grammatical claims.
6. Run cross-testament parallels on the 5 specified passages.

The analysis agent should organize the response around the 14 objections (or group overlapping ones), rating each as STRONG, NUANCED, or TENSION as specified in Focus Area 13. Honest engagement with critics is required — no strawmanning, no motive-questioning.
